description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun with a matte black metal body and wooden accents on the grip and stock. The receiver is rectangular and streamlined, with a cylindrical barrel extending from the front and a simple ring sight at the muzzle. The pistol grip and the rear stock are both clad in wood, providing ergonomic support and a classic aesthetic contrast to the otherwise utilitarian design. A straight box magazine is inserted vertically in front of the trigger guard, and a charging handle is visible on the right side of the receiver. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible rails or advanced attachments, and the iron sights consist of a rear ring and a front post. The construction suggests a robust, functional weapon suitable for close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact submachine gun with a matte black metal body, wooden grip and stock, and a straight box magazine. It features simple iron sights and a utilitarian, robust design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden grip and stock, straight box magazine, and the charging handle on the right side.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular front sight, cylindrical barrel, and the alignment of the magazine below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden grip and stock, with the magazine and barrel clearly visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the alignment of the iron sights, the magazine, and the overall slim profile.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic grip
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple rear ring with fixed front post
